task_completion.description: Quantify tumor signal intensity statistics using the MRI and segmentation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing medical imaging software (e.g., 3D Slicer) or a script execution that calculates the mean, SD, min, and max intensity of the tumor segmentation.
A file manager, terminal, or text editor showing the file `tumor_statistics.csv` located in `~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/` with the calculated statistics.
A file manager or terminal showing the existence of `heterogeneity_report.json` in `~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/`.

task_completion.description: Include basic intensity statistics in the JSON report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of `heterogeneity_report.json` displaying the fields `mean_intensity`, `std_intensity`, `min_intensity`, and `max_intensity` with numerical values.

task_completion.description: Include CV and heterogeneity classification in the JSON report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of `heterogeneity_report.json` displaying the fields `coefficient_of_variation_percent` and `heterogeneity_class` with calculated values.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually computed the statistics from the image data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for evidence of image processing (e.g., using a GUI statistics module or running a Python script with image processing libraries) rather than typing out hardcoded, guessed numbers.

integrity.description: Verify the files were saved to the exact requested directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check file paths in the terminal or file explorer to ensure they are exactly in `~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/` and not just in a temporary or home folder.

integrity.description: Verify the agent saved the files rather than just printing output.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for commands like `cat`, `ls`, or a text editor saving the file, ensuring the JSON and CSV are actually written to disk, not just printed to the console.
